history.content:
<thought>
The agent is at (7,D) facing north. The cell directly in front (6,D) is floor, so it's safe to move forward. The goal is at (5,F). Moving north will bring us closer to the goal, and the path appears clear: after moving north, we can continue north to (5,D) and then east to the goal, avoiding the lava at (6,E) and (6,F). Therefore, the next action is to move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at the bottom center of the view. The goal is at (6,F), two cells east and one cell north. The cell directly ahead (north) at (6,D) is floor, so moving forward is safe and brings us closer to the goal. After moving north, we will then need to turn right and move east to reach the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at (7,D) facing north. The goal is at (7,F), which is two cells to the east. The cell directly ahead is a wall, so I cannot move forward. I need to turn right to face east, then move forward twice to reach the goal, avoiding the lava cells.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at (7,D) facing east. The goal is directly north at (5,D) with a clear path: floor at (6,D) and goal at (5,D). To reach the goal, I should turn left to face north, then move forward twice.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at (7,D) facing north. The goal is at (7,F), two cells to the east. The cell directly north is a wall, so I cannot move forward. I need to turn right to face east, then move forward twice to reach the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at (7,D) facing east. The goal is at (5,D), directly north. The cell north (6,D) is floor, and then the goal. To move north, I need to turn left to face north, then move forward twice. I will turn left now.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The agent is facing north, but the cell directly north is a wall. The goal is at (7,F) two cells east. I need to turn right to face east, then move forward twice to reach the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at (7,D) facing east. The goal is at (5,D) two cells north. The cell directly north (6,D) is a floor cell, so a path exists. I need to turn left to face north, then move forward twice to reach the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at (7,D) facing north. The cell directly north (6,D) is a wall. The goal is at (7,F) two cells east. I need to turn right to face east, then I can move forward twice to reach the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at (7,D) facing east. The goal is at (5,D) directly north. The cell north (6,D) is a floor cell, so we can move north. To face north, we need to turn left. Then we can move forward twice to reach the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at (7,D) facing north. The goal is at (7,F) two cells east. The cell east (7,E) is floor, and east of that is the goal. To face east, I need to turn right.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is at (5, D) directly north of my position. The cell directly in front of me (6, D) is a safe floor cell. I can move forward to approach the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
